gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Missed-interval cap in _calculateIntervalsETHBuild allocates entire pool to one interval, enabling oversized incentive extraction via swapETHForTorusAndBuild
candidate.claimed_root_cause: When missedIntervals >= 1439, _totalAmountForInterval is capped to totalETHForBuild; the caller then receives INCENTIVE_FEE_BUILD (1.5%) of the entire pool as incentive in a single call
candidate.claimed_impact: Unprivileged caller extracts 1.5% of totalETHForBuild (~0.0016 ETH at live state) in one call instead of the intended per-interval fraction (~0.007% of pool)

live_preconditions.name: block.timestamp - lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp >= 1439 * INTERVAL_TIME (863400 seconds, ~10 days)
live_preconditions.observed_value: NOT MET: lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp=1784012400, captured_at=2026-07-14T09:58:29Z (~1784023109), elapsed ~10709 seconds (~178 minutes, ~18 intervals). Cap requires 1439+ missed intervals.

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Computes amountPerInterval as (totalETHForBuild * DAILY_ALLOCATION_ETH_BUILDING / BPS_DENOM) / INTERVALS_PER_DAY, accumulates missed intervals, then caps _totalAmountForInterval to totalETHForBuild. With DAILY_ALLOCATION_ETH_BUILDING=1000 and INTERVALS_PER_DAY=144, cap triggers at missedIntervals >= 1439 (~10 days).

Reads currInterval.amountAllocated (set by modifier from _calculateIntervalsETHBuild), computes incentive = amountAllocated * 150 / 10000, and sends it to msg.sender. If amountAllocated == totalETHForBuild (capped), incentive = 1.5% of entire pool.
INCENTIVE_FEE_BUILD=150, BPS_DENOM=10_000, INTERVALS_PER_DAY=144, INTERVAL_TIME=10 minutes. These confirm the arithmetic in the candidate report.

call_sequence: 1. Wait until block.timestamp - lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp >= 1439 * 600 = 863400 seconds (~10 days with live DAILY_ALLOCATION_ETH_BUILDING=1000)
4. missedIntervals >= 1439 causes _totalAmountForInterval to be capped to totalETHForBuild
6. incentive = totalETHForBuild * 150 / 10000 is transferred to msg.sender
state_after: totalETHForBuild reduced by totalETHForBuild (entire pool consumed in one interval)

extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: ~1599520501288771 wei (~0.0016 ETH) at live state; scales proportionally as totalETHForBuild grows via distributeETHForBuilding
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol loses 1.5% of totalETHForBuild as incentive instead of the intended per-interval fraction (~0.007% of pool per normal call)
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Normal per-interval incentive = (totalETHForBuild * 0.10 / 144) * 0.015 = ~0.00001% of pool. Capped incentive = 1.5% of pool. Ratio ~214x. The IntervalAlreadyUsed guard (line 905) does not block this because the modifier creates a fresh interval with amountBurned=0 before the check executes.

setup: Record attacker ETH balance before call
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If balAfter - balBefore equals only a single-interval normal incentive and not the capped full-pool incentive, the vulnerability does not exist as described.

rejection_reason: Not rejected. Downgraded from HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE to NEEDS_LOCAL_PROOF because the critical temporal precondition is NOT met in the current live state: lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp=1784012400 is only ~10709 seconds (~18 intervals) in the past at capture time, far below the 863400-second (~1439-interval) threshold required to trigger the cap. The code path and arithmetic are confirmed correct from source, but exploitability requires a future state (10 days of no calls) that does not exist now. A pinned-fork test with vm.warp is required to confirm the cap triggers and the incentive transfer executes as described before this can be promoted.
